% Generated by scripts/merge_preambles.py — re-runnable, but edits made
% directly to this file (changing a verbatim, deleting an entry, or
% writing a '% [skip:macro] name' line) ARE the persistent record of
% editorial decisions; the script honours them on the next run.
%
% NOTE: the many '% [skip:macro] ...' lines below were added by a one-off
% reachability sweep (2026-06-02): each names a macro defined in the merged
% union of all papers' sidecars but referenced nowhere in the thesis. The
% skip marker is the durable record; merge_preambles honours it on re-run.

% --- Math operators ---
% [skip:macro] Ber

% [skip:macro] Bin

% [skip:macro] Circ

\DeclareMathOperator{\Cov}{Cov}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2302, arXiv-2305, arXiv-2402, arXiv-2405, arXiv-2406, arXiv-2506

% auto: identical in arXiv-2302, arXiv-2305

\DeclareMathOperator{\Flatten}{flatten}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2506

% [skip:macro] Poi

\DeclareMathOperator{\ReLU}{ReLU}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2506

% [skip:macro] Relu

\DeclareMathOperator{\SoftMax}{softmax}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2506

% picked from arXiv-2402, arXiv-2405, arXiv-2406, arXiv-2506 — conflicts: \newcommand{\Span}{\mathrm{span}} in arXiv-2605

% [skip:macro] Trace

% [skip:macro] Unif

\DeclareMathOperator{\Var}{Var}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2302, arXiv-2305, arXiv-2506

\DeclareMathOperator*{\argmax}{arg\,max}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2605

% [skip:macro] dtv

\DeclareMathOperator{\polylog}{polylog}
% picked from arXiv-2406 — conflicts: \newcommand{\polylog}{\mathrm{polylog}} in arXiv-2605

% [skip:macro] bilingualcommand
% Dead code: paper_parsing.py extracts the \bilingualcommand definition
% from arXiv-2506/custom_latex.tex and arXiv-2605/macros.tex, but it does
% not recognise the calls (\bilingualcommand{\name}{english}{french}) as
% macro definitions. Those calls live only in the upstream sources and
% never reach the build; the resulting bilingual macros (\where, \textif,
% \textand, \textiff, \otherwise) are instead inlined at extraction time
% via arxiv-papers/arXiv-2506-patches.json (and would be in 2605-patches
% if needed). Nothing in the build ever calls \bilingualcommand, so its
% definition is dropped from this file. merge_preambles honours this
% skip marker on re-runs; the upstream sidecars still carry the def
% (harmless — they're inputs only).

\renewcommand{\vec}[1]{{\boldsymbol{#1}}}
% Wrapped in extra braces to isolate as a single math atom. Without them,
% \boldsymbol's amsbsy expansion (\begingroup\boldmath#1\endgroup) leaves
% the inner math atom "open" — so \vec{v^\star}^T in 2402's body triggered
% Double superscript (^\star inside + ^T outside both landed on v). The
% outer {} force a math group, restoring the expected behaviour.
% Conflicts: \bm{#1} in arXiv-2302, arXiv-2405, arXiv-2406, arXiv-2605;
% \boldsymbol{#1} in arXiv-2402, arXiv-2405, arXiv-2406, arXiv-2506;
% \mathbf in arXiv-2402, arXiv-2405; #1 (identity) in arXiv-2305.

% [skip:macro] vv

\newcommand{\vw}{\vec{w}}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2506

\newcommand{\vx}{\vec{x}}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2506

% picked from arXiv-2302 — conflicts: \def\w{w} in arXiv-2305

\def\whp{{\rm w.h.p.}}
% auto: identical in arXiv-2402

% [skip:macro] ww
